description_long: This object consists of two distinct wooden storage boxes, each with a different design and finish. The first box is a dark brown, solid-sided crate with smooth surfaces and integrated oval cutout handles on the shorter sides for easy carrying. The second box is a lighter, natural wood crate with slatted sides and base, providing a more open and airy structure. Both boxes are rectangular in shape and appear to be of similar size, suitable for holding or transporting various items. The construction suggests durability and practical use in urban environments such as garages, markets, or storage rooms. The overall appearance is rustic and functional, with no visible lids or locking mechanisms.
description: A pair of wooden storage boxes: one dark, solid-sided with cutout handles, and one light, slatted and open. Both are rectangular and practical for urban storage or transport.
description_view_0: Front view showing the solid dark box in front and the lighter slatted box behind, highlighting the closed front of the dark box and the open slats of the light box.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the handle cutout on the dark box and the open, stacked slats of the light box, with both boxes side by side.
description_view_2: Rear view with the light slatted box in front and the dark solid box behind, emphasizing the open structure of the slatted box.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handle cutout on the dark box and the slatted construction of the light box, with their positions swapped from view 1.
attribute_handle_design: side-mounted oval cutout handles on the dark box; no handles on the slatted box
